rationale: Both notes indicate a close conceptual link: the 2019 paper studies price framing effects on premium option choice, while the 2000 paper tests additive vs. subtractive option framing on option uptake, spending, and price perceptions in configurable products. Given this strong overlap in framing-based option-choice mechanisms (not a generic background topic), this citation is plausibly foundational/comparative and should be retained for full relation judgment.
